The new strategy from the Philadelphia-based company, which launched in 1992, includes live-streaming the best deals from top stores and an online-only ad that focuses on Black Friday. Last year, the National Retail Federation projected that U.S. shoppers would spend about $3.65 billion on Black Friday and Cyber Monday — the same amount they did in 2014 and about 5 percent less than they did in 2013.
